title: horkelia bolanderi subsp. clevelandii
intro: Horkelia bolanderi subsp. clevelandii, also known as Cleveland's horkelia, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Pyrolaceae. It is native to the western United States and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and combat infections. The plant has been traditionally used to promote overall health and well-being, with potential benefits including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced digestion. Horkelia bolanderi subsp. clevelandii can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for therapeutic purposes.
